title: The Animator’s Survival Kit
creator: Richard Williams
description: A heavily illustrated manual on timing, spacing, walks, runs, weight, dialogue, acting, and the working methods of hand-drawn animation. Its principles transfer readily to 2D and 3D game animation.
useful: The drawings make subtle differences in weight and rhythm easier to see than an abstract list of animation principles.
audience: Beginning animators, gameplay animators, and designers who need to communicate clearly about motion.
learning: Control motion through timing and spacing
Construct convincing walks and changes of weight

manualLinkStatus.note: The official Macmillan page opened in a browser and matched Richard Williams, the edition, page count, and ISBN; automated requests receive 403.

editorial.note: Official edition details verified. Included as adjacent craft knowledge because timing, spacing, weight, and acting directly support gameplay and character animation.
